reasoning_content:
Using `files` parameter works great for multipart form data without needing `requests_toolbelt`. The form fields are correctly sent as multipart/form-data.

Now let me test the actual LipidMaps API to see if it works.

function.name: run_custom_test
function.arguments:
{"code": "import requests
import pandas as pd
from io import StringIO

# Test the actual LipidMaps API
url = "https://www.lipidmaps.org/rest/compound/mass_all"
response = requests.post(url, files={
    'mz': (None, '100.0\n200.0\n300.0'),
    'tolerance': (None, '0.01'),
    'database': (None, 'GLYCOLIPIDS'),
    'adducts': (None, 'M+H,M+Na')
}, timeout=30)
print("Status:", response.status_code)
print("Headers:", dict(response.headers))
print("Response text (first 1000 chars):", response.text[:1000])
", "test_snippet": ""}
